channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump administration to pull thousands of USAID staffers off the job
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cleared the way for one of the Trump administration's remaining steps in its dismantling of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: National Park Service restores some jobs of fired employees, pledges to hire 7,700 seasonal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is restoring jobs for dozens of National Park Service employees fired amid government-wide reductions and hiring nearly 3,000 additional seasonal workers, following an uproar over an aggressive plan to downsize the agency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KTLA 5

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump's second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he may take control of the US Postal Service. Here's what to know
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he may put the U.S. Postal Service under the control of the Commerce Department in what would be an executive branch takeover of the agency, which has operated as an independent entity since 1970.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan illegal immigrants detained at Guantánamo Bay are flown back home
item.description: Flights that left from Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, Thursday transported nearly 200 illegal immigrants detained on the island back to Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 shot and killed outside Louisville, Kentucky, motor vehicle office, police say
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Three people were shot and killed outside a motor vehicle office in Louisville on Friday, police said.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration reverses its previous decision and reinstates legal aid for migrant children
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Days after telling legal groups who help migrant children who arrive in America alone — some so young they are in diapers or their feet dangle from their chairs in court — that they must stop their work, the federal government Friday reversed itself.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Experienced workers, not just rookies, get cut as Trump slashes probationary employees
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Warren Hill spent more than two decades working at the Lake Clark National Park and Preserve, which spans 4 million acres of coastline, forests, lakes and glaciers in Alaska.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connor McDavid scores in OT to give Canada 3-2 win over United States in 4 Nations Face-Off final
item.description: Instead, the Canadian team stood at the blue line, arm in arm, player and coach, wearing their championship hats while the maple leaf flag was lowered behind the 4 Nations Face-Off trophy and the national anthem reverberated across the Americans' home ice.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How LA wildfires are making an already tough rental market even worse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The one-bedroom cottage with a woodsy vibe reminded Heather McAlpine of the home she lost to the brutal Los Angeles-area wildfires. But only two hours after seeing the listing, the rental was snapped up.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Kentucky AG Daniel Cameron launches Senate bid
item.description: Prior to becoming Kentucky’s first Black attorney general, Cameron served as legal counsel to McConnell, the state’s longest serving senator, for two years.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CEO confidence hits highest point in 3 years: Survey
item.description: Forty-four percent in The Conference Board survey said economic conditions have become more preferable “than six months ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 53-car pileup leaves 1 dead in North Carolina, adding to hundreds of snow-related wrecks
item.description: It's a mess out there from the Sandhills to the eastern stretches of the CBS 17 viewing area, causing dozens of wrecks along major and secondary roads in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Drake suprises pregnant fan with VIP tickets and $30,000
item.description: Drake gifted one fan in a major way during a Sydney show on his Anita Max Win Tour in Australia on Wednesday night. A pregnant fan, identified as Tiana Henderson, was sitting in the pit holding a sign that read "I'm 20 weeks pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts event honoring Black History Month
item.description: The celebration comes amid the administration's moves to gut federal diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs and place DEI employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KRON4

item.title: 2 students arrested for allegedly plotting 'mass casualty attack' at Houston school, FBI says
item.description: Two high school students have been arrested for planning what authorities are calling a “mass casualty attack” at a Houston high school.
item.dc:creator: Chad Washington, KRON4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, Fox 5

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter, who forgave gunman's mother, dies at 43
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Anne Marie Hochhalter, who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting but found strength to forgive and to heal her soul after bonding with another family devastated by the tragedy, has died. She was 43. Hochhalter was found in her home in suburban Denver on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
